title: monardella douglasii var. venosa
intro: Monardella douglasii var. venosa, also known as Redstem Monardella, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is characterized by its bright red stems, pink to purple flowers, and distinctive fragrance. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including wound healing and antiseptic effects. Herbal preparations of Monardella douglasii var. venosa can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used to support immune function and alleviate skin conditions.
